papers.title: Geometry-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Multi-view Consistent 3D Scene Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract RL3DEdit uses reinforcement learning with rewards from a 3D foundation model to achieve multi-view consistent 3D editing from 2D editing priors.  					AI-generated summary Leveraging the priors of 2D diffusion models for 3D editing has emerged as a promising paradigm. However, maintaining multi-view consistency in edited results remains challenging, and the extreme scarcity of 3D-consistent editing paired data renders supervised fine-tuning (SFT), the most effective training strategy for editing tasks, infeasible. In this paper, we observe that, while generating multi-view consistent 3D content is highly challenging, verifying 3D consistency is tractable, naturally positioning reinforcement learning (RL) as a feasible solution. Motivated by this, we propose RL3DEdit, a single-pass framework driven by RL optimization with novel rewards derived from the 3D foundation model, VGGT. Specifically, we leverage VGGT's robust priors learned from massive real-world data, feed the edited images, and utilize the output confidence maps and pose estimation errors as reward signals, effectively anchoring the 2D editing priors onto a 3D-consistent manifold via RL. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RL3DEdit achieves stable multi-view consistency and outperforms state-of-the-art methods in editing quality with high efficiency. To promote the development of 3D editing, we will release the code and model.

ru.title: Обучение с подкреплением для многовидовой согласованности в 3D-редактировании
ru.desc: RL3DEdit предлагает инновационный подход к трёхмерному редактированию, используя обучение с подкреплением и награды от 3D фундаментальной модели VGGT для достижения согласованности редактирования во всех видах. Основная проблема заключается в том, что хотя трёхмерное редактирование с использованием прайоров из 2D диффузионных моделей показало перспективность, сохранение многовидовой согласованности остаётся сложным, а недостаток парных данных делает стандартное супервизированное обучение невозможным. Авторы заметили, что проверка 3D-согласованности намного более доступна, чем её генерация, что естественным образом позиционирует обучение с подкреплением как решение данной задачи. Фреймворк использует сигналы вознаграждения из карт уверенности и ошибок оценки позы, эффективно закрепляя 2D-редактирование в трёхмерном многообразии.

en.title: Reinforcement Learning for Consistent 3D Editing
en.desc: RL3DEdit is a novel approach that utilizes reinforcement learning (RL) to enhance 3D editing by ensuring consistency across multiple views. It addresses the challenge of limited 3D-consistent editing data, which makes traditional supervised fine-tuning impractical. By using rewards derived from a 3D foundation model, VGGT, RL3DEdit effectively aligns 2D editing results with a 3D framework. The method has shown to improve editing quality and maintain multi-view consistency, outperforming existing techniques in efficiency and effectiveness.

zh.title: 利用强化学习实现一致的3D编辑
zh.desc: RL3DEdit 是一种利用强化学习的方法，通过从 3D 基础模型中获得奖励，实现从 2D 编辑先验到多视角一致的 3D 编辑。尽管生成多视角一致的 3D 内容非常具有挑战性，但验证 3D 一致性是可行的，这使得强化学习成为一个合适的解决方案。我们提出的 RL3DEdit 框架通过 RL 优化，利用来自 VGGT 的新颖奖励信号，将 2D 编辑先验有效地锚定到 3D 一致的流形上。实验结果表明，RL3DEdit 在编辑质量和效率上均优于现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: Omni-Diffusion: Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Masked Discrete Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract Omni-Diffusion introduces the first any-to-any multimodal language model based on mask-based discrete diffusion models, unifying text, speech, and image processing in a single framework.  					AI-generated summary While recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made impressive strides, they predominantly employ a conventional autoregressive architecture as their backbone, leaving significant room to explore effective and efficient alternatives in architectural design. Concurrently, recent studies have successfully applied discrete diffusion models to various domains, such as visual understanding and image generation, revealing their considerable potential as a promising backbone for multimodal systems. Drawing inspiration from these pioneering research, we introduce Omni-Diffusion, the first any-to-any multimodal language model built entirely on mask-based discrete diffusion models, which unifies understanding and generation across text, speech, and images. Omni-Diffusion employs a unified mask-based discrete diffusion model to directly capture the joint distribution over discrete multimodal tokens. This approach supports not only bimodal tasks but also more complex scenarios involving multiple modalities. On a diverse set of benchmarks, our method outperforms or performs on par with existing multimodal systems that process two or more modalities, highlighting the significant promise of diffusion models in powering the next generation of multimodal foundation models. Project webpage: https://omni-diffusion.github.io.

ru.title: Единая диффузионная архитектура для всех модальностей
ru.desc: Omni-Diffusion представляет первую мультимодальную модель, построенную на основе дискретных диффузионных моделей с маскированием, которая объединяет обработку текста, речи и изображений в единую архитектуру. В отличие от традиционных автарегрессивных больших мультимодальных языковых моделей, это решение использует диффузионный подход для прямого моделирования совместного распределения дискретных токенов разных модальностей. Модель демонстрирует универсальность, поддерживая не только задачи с двумя модальностями, но и более сложные сценарии с множественными модальностями одновременно. На различных бенчмарках Omni-Diffusion показывает результаты, сопоставимые или превосходящие существующие мультимодальные системы, подтверждая потенциал диффузионных моделей как основы для следующего поколения фундаментальных моделей.

en.title: Unifying Multimodal AI with Omni-Diffusion
en.desc: Omni-Diffusion is a groundbreaking multimodal language model that integrates text, speech, and image processing using mask-based discrete diffusion models. Unlike traditional models that rely on autoregressive architectures, this model captures the joint distribution of multimodal tokens effectively. It excels in both bimodal and complex multimodal tasks, showcasing its versatility. The results demonstrate that Omni-Diffusion outperforms or matches existing systems, indicating the potential of diffusion models in advancing multimodal AI applications.

zh.title: Omni-Diffusion：统一多模态处理的新纪元
zh.desc: Omni-Diffusion 是首个基于掩码离散扩散模型的多模态语言模型，能够处理文本、语音和图像。该模型通过统一的掩码离散扩散模型，直接捕捉多模态离散标记的联合分布。与传统的自回归架构相比，Omni-Diffusion 在多模态任务中表现出色，支持更复杂的多模态场景。实验结果表明，该方法在多个基准测试中超越或与现有多模态系统持平，展示了扩散模型在多模态基础模型中的巨大潜力。

papers.title: Thinking to Recall: How Reasoning Unlocks Parametric Knowledge in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Reasoning in large language models enhances parametric knowledge recall through computational buffer and factual priming mechanisms, though it carries risks of hallucination that can be mitigated by prioritizing accurate reasoning trajectories.  					AI-generated summary While reasoning in LLMs plays a natural role in math, code generation, and multi-hop factual questions, its effect on simple, single-hop factual questions remains unclear. Such questions do not require step-by-step logical decomposition, making the utility of reasoning highly counterintuitive. Nevertheless, we find that enabling reasoning substantially expands the capability boundary of the model's parametric knowledge recall, unlocking correct answers that are otherwise effectively unreachable. Why does reasoning aid parametric knowledge recall when there are no complex reasoning steps to be done? To answer this, we design a series of hypothesis-driven controlled experiments, and identify two key driving mechanisms: (1) a computational buffer effect, where the model uses the generated reasoning tokens to perform latent computation independent of their semantic content; and (2) factual priming, where generating topically related facts acts as a semantic bridge that facilitates correct answer retrieval. Importantly, this latter generative self-retrieval mechanism carries inherent risks: we demonstrate that hallucinating intermediate facts during reasoning increases the likelihood of hallucinations in the final answer. Finally, we show that our insights can be harnessed to directly improve model accuracy by prioritizing reasoning trajectories that contain hallucination-free factual statements.

ru.title: Рассуждение как вычислительный буфер для разблокировки скрытого знания
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что рассуждение в больших языковых моделях значительно улучшает извлечение параметрического знания даже для простых одношаговых фактических вопросов. Авторы выявили два ключевых механизма: вычислительный буфер, где модель использует сгенерированные токены для скрытого вычисления, и фактическую грунтовку, где релевантные факты помогают достичь правильный ответ. Однако это также увеличивает риск галлюцинаций промежуточных фактов, что приводит к ошибкам в финальном ответе. Разработанный метод приоритизации безошибочных траекторий рассуждения позволяет значительно повысить точность модели.

en.title: Unlocking Knowledge Recall Through Reasoning in LLMs
en.desc: This paper explores how reasoning in large language models (LLMs) can improve their ability to recall factual knowledge, even for simple questions that don't require complex reasoning. The authors identify two main mechanisms that enhance this recall: a computational buffer effect, which allows the model to perform calculations using reasoning tokens, and factual priming, where related facts help retrieve correct answers. However, the study also highlights the risk of hallucinations, where the model generates incorrect information, which can lead to errors in the final answer. By focusing on reasoning paths that avoid hallucinations, the researchers propose methods to enhance the accuracy of LLMs.

zh.title: 推理提升知识回忆，助力模型准确性
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）中的推理能力如何通过计算缓冲和事实引导机制增强参数知识的回忆。尽管在简单的单步事实问题中，推理的效果并不明显，但研究发现启用推理可以显著扩展模型的知识回忆能力。论文设计了一系列实验，揭示了推理对知识回忆的影响机制，包括计算缓冲效应和事实引导。最后，研究表明，通过优先考虑无幻觉的推理路径，可以直接提高模型的准确性。

papers.title: MM-Zero: Self-Evolving Multi-Model Vision Language Models From Zero Data
papers.abstract: Abstract MM-Zero enables zero-data self-evolution of vision-language models through a multi-role framework with proposer, coder, and solver components trained via group relative policy optimization.  					AI-generated summary Self-evolving has emerged as a key paradigm for improving foundational models such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) with minimal human intervention. While recent approaches have demonstrated that LLM agents can self-evolve from scratch with little to no data, VLMs introduce an additional visual modality that typically requires at least some seed data, such as images, to bootstrap the self-evolution process. In this work, we present Multi-model Multimodal Zero (MM-Zero), the first RL-based framework to achieve zero-data self-evolution for VLM reasoning. Moving beyond prior dual-role (Proposer and Solver) setups, MM-Zero introduces a multi-role self-evolving training framework comprising three specialized roles: a Proposer that generates abstract visual concepts and formulates questions; a Coder that translates these concepts into executable code (e.g., Python, SVG) to render visual images; and a Solver that performs multimodal reasoning over the generated visual content. All three roles are initialized from the same base model and trained using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), with carefully designed reward mechanisms that integrate execution feedback, visual verification, and difficulty balancing. Our experiments show that MM-Zero improves VLM reasoning performance across a wide range of multimodal benchmarks. MM-Zero establishes a scalable path toward self-evolving multi-model systems for multimodal models, extending the frontier of self-improvement beyond the conventional two-model paradigm.

ru.title: Самоэволюция мультимодальных моделей без данных через многоролевую оптимизацию
ru.desc: MM-Zero представляет инновационный RL-based фреймворк для самообучения vision-language моделей без использования данных. Система использует многоролевую архитектуру с тремя компонентами: Proposer генерирует визуальные концепции и вопросы, Coder преобразует их в исполняемый код для визуализации, а Solver выполняет мультимодальное рассуждение над созданным контентом. Все компоненты инициализируются из одной базовой модели и тренируются методом Group Relative Policy Optimization с интегрированными механизмами вознаграждения. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют значительное улучшение производительности VLM на различных мультимодальных бенчмарках.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with Zero-Data Self-Evolution
en.desc: MM-Zero is a novel framework that enables vision-language models to evolve without any initial data. It introduces a multi-role system consisting of a Proposer, Coder, and Solver, each playing a specific role in generating and processing visual concepts. The framework utilizes Group Relative Policy Optimization to train these roles, allowing them to learn from execution feedback and visual verification. This approach significantly enhances the reasoning capabilities of vision-language models across various multimodal tasks, paving the way for more advanced self-evolving AI systems.

zh.title: 无数据自我进化的视觉-语言模型
zh.desc: MM-Zero 是一种新型的视觉-语言模型自我进化框架，能够在没有数据的情况下进行自我演化。它通过引入提议者、编码器和求解器三个角色，利用群体相对策略优化进行训练。提议者生成抽象的视觉概念并提出问题，编码器将这些概念转化为可执行的代码，而求解器则对生成的视觉内容进行多模态推理。实验结果表明，MM-Zero 在多种多模态基准测试中提升了视觉-语言模型的推理性能。

papers.title: InternVL-U: Democratizing Unified Multimodal Models for Understanding, Reasoning, Generation and Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract InternVL-U is a 4-billion parameter unified multimodal model that combines advanced visual generation with robust semantic understanding through specialized modular design and reasoning-centric data synthesis.  					AI-generated summary Unified multimodal models (UMMs) that integrate understanding, reasoning, generation, and editing face inherent trade-offs between maintaining strong semantic comprehension and acquiring powerful generation capabilities. In this report, we present InternVL-U, a lightweight 4B-parameter UMM that democratizes these capabilities within a unified framework. Guided by the principles of unified contextual modeling and modality-specific modular design with decoupled visual representations, InternVL-U integrates a state-of-the-art Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) with a specialized MMDiT-based visual generation head. To further bridge the gap between aesthetic generation and high-level intelligence, we construct a comprehensive data synthesis pipeline targeting high-semantic-density tasks, such as text rendering and scientific reasoning, under a reasoning-centric paradigm that leverages Chain-of-Thought (CoT) to better align abstract user intent with fine-grained visual generation details. Extensive experiments demonstrate that InternVL-U achieves a superior performance - efficiency balance. Despite using only 4B parameters, it consistently outperforms unified baseline models with over 3x larger scales such as BAGEL (14B) on various generation and editing tasks, while retaining strong multimodal understanding and reasoning capabilities.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Четырёхмиллиардная модель для понимания и создания мультимодального контента
ru.desc: InternVL-U — это компактная мультимодальная языковая модель с 4 миллиардами параметров, которая объединяет способности к пониманию изображений и текста с генерацией визуального контента в единую архитектуру. Модель использует модульный дизайн с отдельными компонентами для различных задач, включая специализированный визуальный генератор на основе MMDiT. Авторы разработали инновационный конвейер синтеза данных, ориентированный на высокосемантические задачи, такие как распознавание текста в изображениях и научные рассуждения, с применением цепочки мыслей (Chain-of-Thought). Несмотря на компактный размер, модель превосходит существенно более крупные базовые модели вроде BAGEL (14B) по качеству генерации и редактирования, сохраняя сильные способности к пониманию и рассуждению.

en.title: InternVL-U: Bridging Visual Generation and Semantic Understanding Efficiently
en.desc: InternVL-U is a unified multimodal model designed to enhance both visual generation and semantic understanding with only 4 billion parameters. It employs a modular architecture that allows for specialized processing of different data types, improving its ability to reason and generate content effectively. The model integrates a Multimodal Large Language Model with a visual generation component, utilizing a data synthesis pipeline that focuses on high-level tasks like text rendering and scientific reasoning. Through extensive testing, InternVL-U demonstrates superior performance and efficiency, outperforming larger models while maintaining strong multimodal capabilities.

zh.title: 统一多模态模型的创新之路
zh.desc: InternVL-U是一个拥有40亿参数的统一多模态模型，结合了先进的视觉生成和强大的语义理解。该模型通过模块化设计和以推理为中心的数据合成，解决了多模态模型在语义理解和生成能力之间的权衡。InternVL-U采用了最新的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）和专门的视觉生成模块，能够在高语义密度任务中表现出色。实验结果表明，尽管参数量较少，InternVL-U在生成和编辑任务上仍然超越了规模更大的基线模型。

papers.title: Stepping VLMs onto the Court: Benchmarking Spatial Intelligence in Sports
papers.abstract: Abstract CourtSI is a large-scale spatial intelligence dataset for sports scenarios that enables evaluation and improvement of vision-language models' understanding of human motion and object interactions.  					AI-generated summary Sports have long attracted broad attention as they push the limits of human physical and cognitive capabilities. Amid growing interest in spatial intelligence for vision-language models (VLMs), sports provide a natural testbed for understanding high-intensity human motion and dynamic object interactions. To this end, we present CourtSI, the first large-scale spatial intelligence dataset tailored to sports scenarios. CourtSI contains over 1M QA pairs, organized under a holistic taxonomy that systematically covers spatial counting, distance measurement, localization, and relational reasoning, across representative net sports including badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Leveraging well-defined court geometry as metric anchors, we develop a semi-automatic data engine to reconstruct sports scenes, enabling scalable curation of CourtSI. In addition, we introduce CourtSI-Bench, a high-quality evaluation benchmark comprising 3,686 QA pairs with rigorous human verification. We evaluate 25 proprietary and open-source VLMs on CourtSI-Bench, revealing a remaining human-AI performance gap and limited generalization from existing spatial intelligence benchmarks. These findings indicate that sports scenarios expose limitations in spatial intelligence capabilities captured by existing benchmarks. Further, fine-tuning Qwen3-VL-8B on CourtSI improves accuracy on CourtSI-Bench by 23.5 percentage points. The adapted model also generalizes effectively to CourtSI-Ext, an evaluation set built on a similar but unseen sport, and demonstrates enhanced spatial-aware commentary generation. Together, these findings demonstrate that CourtSI provides a scalable pathway toward advancing spatial intelligence of VLMs in sports.
affiliations: East China University of Science and Technology
Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Пространственный интеллект видео-языковых моделей через спортивные сценарии
ru.desc: Представлена CourtSI — первый крупномасштабный датасет для оценки пространственного интеллекта видео-языковых моделей на примере спортивных сценариев. Датасет содержит более 1 миллиона пар вопрос-ответ, охватывающих задачи подсчёта объектов, измерения расстояний, локализации и пространственного рассуждения в сетевых видах спорта. Авторы разработали полуавтоматический конвейер обработки данных, использующий геометрию спортивного поля как метрические якоря для масштабируемой аннотации. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительный разрыв между производительностью моделей и человека, а также демонстрируют улучшение на 23,5 процентных пункта при дообучении на предложенном датасете.

en.title: Advancing AI's Spatial Intelligence in Sports with CourtSI
en.desc: CourtSI is a new dataset designed to improve how vision-language models (VLMs) understand sports scenarios, focusing on human motion and object interactions. It includes over 1 million question-answer pairs that cover various spatial reasoning tasks like counting and measuring distances in sports like badminton and tennis. The dataset is created using a semi-automatic process that reconstructs sports scenes based on court geometry, making it easier to gather data. Evaluations show that fine-tuning models on CourtSI significantly enhances their performance, highlighting the need for better spatial intelligence in AI when dealing with dynamic sports environments.

zh.title: CourtSI：提升体育场景下的空间智能
zh.desc: CourtSI是一个针对体育场景的大规模空间智能数据集，旨在评估和提升视觉语言模型对人类运动和物体交互的理解能力。该数据集包含超过100万个问答对，涵盖空间计数、距离测量、定位和关系推理等方面，适用于羽毛球、网球和乒乓球等代表性网球运动。通过利用明确的场地几何形状作为度量锚点，CourtSI实现了体育场景的半自动重建，便于大规模数据的整理。此外，研究还展示了在CourtSI-Bench上对25个视觉语言模型的评估，揭示了人类与AI之间的性能差距，表明现有基准在空间智能能力上存在局限。

papers.title: Reading, Not Thinking: Understanding and Bridging the Modality Gap When Text Becomes Pixels in Multimodal LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Multimodal large language models exhibit inconsistent performance when processing text from images versus textual tokens, with factors like rendering quality and task type influencing this modality gap, which can be mitigated through self-distillation techniques that leverage text-based reasoning traces.  					AI-generated summary Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can process text presented as images, yet they often perform worse than when the same content is provided as textual tokens. We systematically diagnose this "modality gap" by evaluating seven MLLMs across seven benchmarks in five input modes, spanning both synthetically rendered text and realistic document images from arXiv PDFs to Wikipedia pages. We find that the modality gap is task- and data-dependent. For example, math tasks degrade by over 60 points on synthetic renderings, while natural document images often match or exceed text-mode performance. Rendering choices such as font and resolution are strong confounds, with font alone swinging accuracy by up to 47 percentage points. To understand this, we conduct a grounded-theory error analysis of over 4,000 examples, revealing that image mode selectively amplifies reading errors (calculation and formatting failures) while leaving knowledge and reasoning errors largely unchanged, and that some models exhibit a chain-of-thought reasoning collapse under visual input. Motivated by these findings, we propose a self-distillation method that trains the model on its own pure text reasoning traces paired with image inputs, raising image-mode accuracy on GSM8K from 30.71% to 92.72% and transferring to unseen benchmarks without catastrophic forgetting. Overall, our study provides a systematic understanding of the modality gap and suggests a practical path toward improving visual text understanding in multimodal language models.

ru.title: Преодоление модального разрыва в мультимодальных моделях через self-distillation
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема неконсистентной работы мультимодальных больших языковых моделей при обработке текста из изображений по сравнению с текстовыми токенами. Авторы систематически диагностируют этот «модальный разрыв» путём оценки семи моделей на семи бенчмарках в пяти режимах входа, анализируя синтетический текст и реальные документы. Через анализ ошибок обнаружено, что режим изображения усиливает ошибки чтения (вычислительные и форматные ошибки), в то время как ошибки знаний остаются неизменными. Предложен метод self-distillation, который обучает модель на собственных текстовых рассуждениях в паре с визуальными входами, значительно повышая точность на задачах типа GSM8K.

en.title: Bridging the Modality Gap in Multimodal Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates the performance differences of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) when processing text from images compared to text tokens. It identifies a 'modality gap' influenced by factors such as rendering quality and task type, revealing that certain tasks, like math, suffer significantly when using synthetic images. The authors conduct a thorough analysis of errors, finding that image inputs can exacerbate reading mistakes while not affecting knowledge errors. To address these issues, they propose a self-distillation technique that enhances model accuracy on image inputs, demonstrating significant improvements in performance across various benchmarks.

zh.title: 提升多模态语言模型的视觉文本理解
zh.desc: 多模态大型语言模型在处理图像中的文本时，表现通常不如处理文本标记时的效果。这种“模态差距”受到渲染质量和任务类型的影响，且在不同的输入模式下表现不一。通过对超过4000个示例的错误分析，我们发现图像模式会放大阅读错误，而知识和推理错误则相对不变。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种自蒸馏方法，通过训练模型在图像输入下的纯文本推理轨迹，从而显著提高了图像模式的准确性。

papers.title: VLM-SubtleBench: How Far Are VLMs from Human-Level Subtle Comparative Reasoning?
papers.abstract: Abstract VLM-SubtleBench is introduced as a benchmark for evaluating vision-language models on subtle comparative reasoning across diverse domains, revealing significant gaps between model and human performance.  					AI-generated summary The ability to distinguish subtle differences between visually similar images is essential for diverse domains such as industrial anomaly detection, medical imaging, and aerial surveillance. While comparative reasoning benchmarks for vision-language models (VLMs) have recently emerged, they primarily focus on images with large, salient differences and fail to capture the nuanced reasoning required for real-world applications. In this work, we introduce VLM-SubtleBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate VLMs on subtle comparative reasoning. Our benchmark covers ten difference types - Attribute, State, Emotion, Temporal, Spatial, Existence, Quantity, Quality, Viewpoint, and Action - and curate paired question-image sets reflecting these fine-grained variations. Unlike prior benchmarks restricted to natural image datasets, our benchmark spans diverse domains, including industrial, aerial, and medical imagery. Through extensive evaluation of both proprietary and open-source VLMs, we reveal systematic gaps between model and human performance across difference types and domains, and provide controlled analyses highlighting where VLMs' reasoning sharply deteriorates. Together, our benchmark and findings establish a foundation for advancing VLMs toward human-level comparative reasoning.

ru.title: Обучение моделям видеть тонкие различия: бенчмарк для сравнительного мышления
ru.desc: Представлена VLM-SubtleBench - новый бенчмарк для оценки мультимодальных моделей зрения и языка на задачах тонкого сравнительного анализа изображений. Бенчмарк охватывает десять типов различий между визуально похожими изображениями (атрибуты, состояние, эмоции, временные и пространственные характеристики) и включает разнообразные домены: промышленные, аэрофотосъемку и медицинскую визуализацию. Авторы выявили значительные пробелы между производительностью современных VLM и человеческими способностями в распознавании тонких деталей. Исследование предоставляет анализ ограничений моделей и создает основу для развития более совершенных систем понимания изображений.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Enhancing VLMs for Subtle Comparative Reasoning
en.desc: VLM-SubtleBench is a new benchmark created to test vision-language models (VLMs) on their ability to perform subtle comparative reasoning. This benchmark is important because it focuses on distinguishing small differences in images, which is crucial for fields like medical imaging and industrial inspections. Unlike previous benchmarks that only looked at obvious differences, VLM-SubtleBench includes ten types of subtle differences, such as emotion and quantity, across various domains. The study shows that there are significant gaps between how well VLMs perform compared to humans, highlighting areas where these models need improvement.

zh.title: 提升视觉语言模型的细微比较推理能力
zh.desc: VLM-SubtleBench是一个用于评估视觉语言模型在细微比较推理方面的基准。该基准揭示了模型与人类表现之间的显著差距，尤其是在处理视觉相似图像时。它涵盖了十种差异类型，包括属性、状态、情感等，旨在反映现实应用中所需的细致推理。通过对多种视觉语言模型的广泛评估，我们发现模型在不同类型和领域的推理能力存在系统性不足。

papers.title: Do What I Say: A Spoken Prompt Dataset for Instruction-Following
papers.abstract: Abstract Speech Large Language Models are evaluated using a new multilingual dataset that pairs spoken and written prompts across multiple tasks and languages, revealing that text prompts generally outperform spoken prompts except in speech-output tasks.  					AI-generated summary Speech Large Language Models (SLLMs) have rapidly expanded, supporting a wide range of tasks. These models are typically evaluated using text prompts, which may not reflect real-world scenarios where users interact with speech. To address this gap, we introduce DoWhatISay (DOWIS), a multilingual dataset of human-recorded spoken and written prompts designed to pair with any existing benchmark for realistic evaluation of SLLMs under spoken instruction conditions. Spanning 9 tasks and 11 languages, it provides 10 prompt variants per task-language pair, across five styles. Using DOWIS, we benchmark state-of-the-art SLLMs, analyzing the interplay between prompt modality, style, language, and task type. Results show that text prompts consistently outperform spoken prompts, particularly for low-resource and cross-lingual settings. Only for tasks with speech output, spoken prompts do close the gap, highlighting the need for speech-based prompting in SLLM evaluation.

ru.title: Когда текст лучше звука: оценка речевых моделей через многоязычный датасет
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая многоязычная оценочная база данных DoWhatISay, которая содержит пары устных и письменных инструкций для оценки речевых большых языковых моделей. Исследователи провели бенчмарк современных моделей на 9 задачах и 11 языках, анализируя влияние модальности, стиля и языка на качество выполнения. Результаты показывают, что текстовые инструкции обычно дают лучшие результаты, чем устные, особенно для низкоресурсных и кросслингвальных сценариев. Исключение составляют задачи с речевым выходом, где устные инструкции сближают разрыв в производительности между модальностями.

en.title: Evaluating Speech Models: Text vs. Speech Prompts
en.desc: This paper introduces a new multilingual dataset called DoWhatISay (DOWIS) for evaluating Speech Large Language Models (SLLMs) using both spoken and written prompts. The dataset includes human-recorded prompts across 9 tasks and 11 languages, allowing for a realistic assessment of SLLMs in scenarios where users might use speech. The findings indicate that text prompts generally perform better than spoken prompts, except in tasks that specifically require speech output. This highlights the importance of incorporating speech-based evaluations to better understand the capabilities of SLLMs in real-world applications.

zh.title: 语音提示与文本提示的评估对比
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种新的多语言数据集，名为DoWhatISay（DOWIS），用于评估语音大型语言模型（SLLMs）。研究发现，文本提示通常在大多数任务中优于语音提示，尤其是在低资源和跨语言的情况下。DOWIS数据集包含人类录制的语音和书面提示，涵盖9个任务和11种语言，提供每个任务-语言对的10种提示变体。该研究强调了在语音输出任务中，语音提示的重要性，表明在评估SLLMs时需要考虑语音提示。

papers.title: MiniAppBench: Evaluating the Shift from Text to Interactive HTML Responses in LLM-Powered Assistants
papers.abstract: Abstract MiniAppBench introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating principle-driven, interactive application generation, addressing the gap in existing benchmarks that focus on static correctness rather than dynamic, real-world interactions.  					AI-generated summary With the rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) in code generation, human-AI interaction is evolving from static text responses to dynamic, interactive HTML-based applications, which we term MiniApps. These applications require models to not only render visual interfaces but also construct customized interaction logic that adheres to real-world principles. However, existing benchmarks primarily focus on algorithmic correctness or static layout reconstruction, failing to capture the capabilities required for this new paradigm. To address this gap, we introduce MiniAppBench, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate principle-driven, interactive application generation. Sourced from a real-world application with 10M+ generations, MiniAppBench distills 500 tasks across six domains (e.g., Games, Science, and Tools). Furthermore, to tackle the challenge of evaluating open-ended interactions where no single ground truth exists, we propose MiniAppEval, an agentic evaluation framework. Leveraging browser automation, it performs human-like exploratory testing to systematically assess applications across three dimensions: Intention, Static, and Dynamic. Our experiments reveal that current LLMs still face significant challenges in generating high-quality MiniApps, while MiniAppEval demonstrates high alignment with human judgment, establishing a reliable standard for future research. Our code is available in github.com/MiniAppBench.

ru.title: Оценка интерактивных приложений за пределами статической корректности
ru.desc: MiniAppBench — первый комплексный бенчмарк для оценки генерации интерактивных приложений на основе принципов, созданный на основе реальных данных с 500 задачами в шести доменах. Авторы предлагают MiniAppEval — агентное фреймворк для оценки открытых взаимодействий, используя автоматизацию браузера и методы, напоминающие человеческое тестирование. Оценка проводится по трём измерениям: намерение пользователя, статические свойства и динамическое поведение приложения. Эксперименты показывают, что современные LLM по-прежнему испытывают значительные сложности при генерации высококачественных интерактивных приложений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Interactive Application Generation with MiniAppBench
en.desc: MiniAppBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate the generation of interactive applications using Large Language Models (LLMs). Unlike previous benchmarks that focused on static correctness, MiniAppBench assesses the dynamic interactions and real-world principles necessary for creating functional MiniApps. It includes 500 tasks across various domains and introduces MiniAppEval, a framework for evaluating applications through human-like exploratory testing. The findings indicate that while LLMs are improving, they still struggle with producing high-quality interactive applications, highlighting the need for better evaluation standards.

zh.title: 评估交互式应用生成的新基准
zh.desc: MiniAppBench是第一个全面的基准测试，用于评估基于原则的交互式应用程序生成，填补了现有基准测试的空白。现有的基准主要关注算法的正确性或静态布局重建，而MiniAppBench则关注动态的、真实世界的交互。该基准从一个拥有超过1000万生成的真实应用中提取了500个任务，涵盖六个领域。为了评估开放式交互的挑战，我们提出了MiniAppEval，一个基于浏览器自动化的评估框架，能够系统地评估应用程序的意图、静态和动态表现。

papers.title: Fish Audio S2 Technical Report
papers.abstract: Abstract Fish Audio S2 is an open-source text-to-speech system with multi-speaker capabilities, multi-turn generation, and instruction-following control through natural-language descriptions, utilizing a multi-stage training approach and production-ready inference engine.  					AI-generated summary We introduce Fish Audio S2, an open-sourced text-to-speech system featuring multi-speaker, multi-turn generation, and, most importantly, instruction-following control via natural-language descriptions. To scale training, we develop a multi-stage training recipe together with a staged data pipeline covering video captioning and speech captioning, voice-quality assessment, and reward modeling. To push the frontier of open-source TTS, we release our model weights, fine-tuning code, and an SGLang-based inference engine. The inference engine is production-ready for streaming, achieving an RTF of 0.195 and a time-to-first-audio below 100 ms.Our code and weights are available on GitHub (https://github.com/fishaudio/fish-speech) and Hugging Face (https://huggingface.co/fishaudio/s2-pro). We highly encourage readers to visit https://fish.audio to try custom voices.

ru.title: Синтез речи нескольких дикторов с управлением через естественный язык
ru.desc: Fish Audio S2 — это открытая система синтеза речи из текста с поддержкой множества дикторов и многоходового генерирования. Система позволяет управлять процессом генерации через естественный язык, следуя инструкциям пользователя. Для обучения модели применяется многоэтапный подход с конвейером данных, включающим разметку видео и аудио, оценку качества голоса и обучение reward-моделей. Модель оптимизирована для продакшена с скоростью обработки 0.195 RTF и задержкой до первого аудио менее 100 миллисекунд.

en.title: Revolutionizing Text-to-Speech with Fish Audio S2
en.desc: Fish Audio S2 is an advanced open-source text-to-speech (TTS) system that supports multiple speakers and can generate speech in response to natural language instructions. It employs a multi-stage training approach that includes video and speech captioning, as well as voice quality assessment and reward modeling to enhance performance. The system is designed for production use, featuring a fast inference engine with a real-time factor (RTF) of 0.195 and quick audio output. The model and its components are publicly available for developers to use and customize, promoting further innovation in TTS technology.

zh.title: 开源文本到语音系统，体验多样化声音！
zh.desc: Fish Audio S2 是一个开源的文本到语音系统，具有多说话者和多轮生成的能力。它通过自然语言描述实现指令跟随控制，提升了用户交互体验。该系统采用多阶段训练方法，并配备了适合生产环境的推理引擎，支持流式处理。我们还发布了模型权重、微调代码和基于 SGLang 的推理引擎，鼓励用户访问网站尝试自定义声音。

papers.title: Are Audio-Language Models Listening? Audio-Specialist Heads for Adaptive Audio Steering
papers.abstract: Abstract Mechanistic interpretability identifies audio-specialist attention heads in large audio-language models to enhance audio utilization through activation interventions at inference time.  					AI-generated summary Multimodal large language models can exhibit text dominance, over-relying on linguistic priors instead of grounding predictions in non-text inputs. One example is large audio-language models (LALMs) where decisive audio evidence can be under-utilized even when it contains important information. To address this issue we use mechanistic interpretability to identify a small set of audio-specialist attention heads whose audio attention yields a ``listening'' signal. We show that this signal increases when audio evidence affects the model's output, providing an indicator of audio engagement under standard prompting. Leveraging this localization, we construct an audio--silence steering direction and apply an inference-time activation intervention to the final representation, amplifying the model's audio effect. To demonstrate the utility of this intervention, we show on MMAU that this improves accuracy by up to +8.0 percentage points on two Qwen-based LALMs, without any parameter updates.

ru.title: Заставим модель слушать: интервенции в специализированные головы внимания для аудиоориентированности
ru.desc: Исследование использует механистическую интерпретируемость для выявления в больших аудиоязыковых моделях специализированных голов внимания, которые фокусируются на аудиоинформации. Авторы показывают, что эти головы внимания генерируют сигнал «слушания», который усиливается когда аудиоданные влияют на выходные предсказания модели. На основе локализованных голов внимания они создают направление сдвига и применяют интервенцию активации во время инференса для усиления влияния аудиоинформации. Метод улучшает точность на тестовом наборе MMAU на 8 процентных пункктов без обновления параметров модели.

en.title: Enhancing Audio Engagement in Language Models
en.desc: This paper focuses on improving how large audio-language models (LALMs) use audio information during inference. It identifies specific attention heads in the model that specialize in processing audio, which helps to enhance the model's engagement with audio inputs. By applying activation interventions based on these identified attention heads, the authors demonstrate a significant increase in the model's accuracy when making predictions that involve audio evidence. This approach allows the model to better utilize audio data without needing to change its underlying parameters.

zh.title: 提升音频利用率，增强模型表现
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了如何通过机械解释性来提高大型音频语言模型的音频利用率。研究者识别出了一小部分专注于音频的注意力头，这些注意力头在模型输出中提供了“倾听”信号。通过在推理时进行激活干预，增强了模型对音频信息的响应，从而提高了模型的准确性。实验结果显示，在不更新参数的情况下，准确率提高了多达8个百分点。

papers.title: Streaming Autoregressive Video Generation via Diagonal Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract Diagonal Distillation improves video generation speed and quality by leveraging temporal context and asymmetric denoising steps while addressing error accumulation and motion coherence issues in diffusion model distillation.  					AI-generated summary Large pretrained diffusion models have significantly enhanced the quality of generated videos, and yet their use in real-time streaming remains limited. Autoregressive models offer a natural framework for sequential frame synthesis but require heavy computation to achieve high fidelity. Diffusion distillation can compress these models into efficient few-step variants, but existing video distillation approaches largely adapt image-specific methods that neglect temporal dependencies. These techniques often excel in image generation but underperform in video synthesis, exhibiting reduced motion coherence, error accumulation over long sequences, and a latency-quality trade-off. We identify two factors that result in these limitations: insufficient utilization of temporal context during step reduction and implicit prediction of subsequent noise levels in next-chunk prediction (i.e., exposure bias). To address these issues, we propose Diagonal Distillation, which operates orthogonally to existing approaches and better exploits temporal information across both video chunks and denoising steps. Central to our approach is an asymmetric generation strategy: more steps early, fewer steps later. This design allows later chunks to inherit rich appearance information from thoroughly processed early chunks, while using partially denoised chunks as conditional inputs for subsequent synthesis. By aligning the implicit prediction of subsequent noise levels during chunk generation with the actual inference conditions, our approach mitigates error propagation and reduces oversaturation in long-range sequences. We further incorporate implicit optical flow modeling to preserve motion quality under strict step constraints. Our method generates a 5-second video in 2.61 seconds (up to 31 FPS), achieving a 277.3x speedup over the undistilled model.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Диагональная дистилляция: временные зависимости и асимметричный денойзинг для быстрой генерации видео
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Diagonal Distillation для ускорения генерации видео с помощью диффузионных моделей. Ключевая идея — использовать асимметричную стратегию дефаззификации: больше шагов шумоудаления в начале видеопоследовательности и меньше в конце, что позволяет накапливать визуальную информацию. Метод явно моделирует временные зависимости между кадрами и управляет ошибками предсказания уровня шума (exposure bias), включая оптический поток для сохранения качества движения. Результаты показывают ускорение в 277 раз с достижением 31 FPS при сохранении высокого качества видео.

en.title: Speeding Up Video Generation with Diagonal Distillation
en.desc: The paper introduces Diagonal Distillation, a novel approach to enhance video generation speed and quality by effectively utilizing temporal context and asymmetric denoising steps. It addresses common issues in diffusion model distillation, such as error accumulation and motion coherence, which are often overlooked in traditional image-focused methods. By implementing a strategy that allows for more denoising steps at the beginning and fewer later, the method ensures that later video chunks benefit from detailed information from earlier ones. This results in a significant speedup in video generation, achieving up to 31 frames per second while maintaining high fidelity and coherence in the generated sequences.

zh.title: 对角蒸馏：提升视频生成速度与质量的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为对角蒸馏（Diagonal Distillation）的方法，旨在提高视频生成的速度和质量。该方法通过利用时间上下文和不对称去噪步骤，解决了扩散模型蒸馏中的错误累积和运动一致性问题。对角蒸馏采用了早期生成更多步骤、后期生成更少步骤的策略，使得后续视频片段能够继承早期片段的丰富信息。最终，该方法在生成5秒视频时仅需2.61秒，速度比未蒸馏模型快277.3倍。

papers.title: Decoupling Reasoning and Confidence: Resurrecting Calibration in Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
papers.abstract: Abstract DCPO framework decouples reasoning and calibration objectives in LLMs to address calibration degeneration while maintaining high accuracy.  					AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances large language models (LLMs) reasoning but severely suffers from calibration degeneration, where models become excessively over-confident in incorrect answers. Previous studies devote to directly incorporating calibration objective into existing optimization target. However, our theoretical analysis demonstrates that there exists a fundamental gradient conflict between the optimization for maximizing policy accuracy and minimizing calibration error. Building on this insight, we propose DCPO, a simple yet effective framework that systematically decouples reasoning and calibration objectives. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DCPO not only preserves accuracy on par with GRPO but also achieves the best calibration performance and substantially mitigates the over-confidence issue. Our study provides valuable insights and practical solution for more reliable LLM deployment.
affiliations: Chinese Information Processing Laboratory, Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Разделение целей: путь к надёжной калибровке языковых моделей
ru.desc: Авторы исследуют проблему калибровки в больших языковых моделях, обученных методом обучения с подкреплением на проверяемых вознаграждениях, и показывают, что прямое объединение целей повышения точности и улучшения калибровки приводит к конфликту градиентов. Они предлагают DCPO — новую методику, которая разделяет задачу рассуждения и задачу калибровки в отдельные оптимизационные процессы. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что DCPO сохраняет высокую точность модели, одновременно достигая лучшей калибровки и снижая избыточную уверенность в неправильных ответах. Эта работа предоставляет практическое решение для более надёжного развёртывания языковых моделей в реальных приложениях.

en.title: Decoupling Reasoning and Calibration for Reliable LLMs
en.desc: The DCPO framework addresses the problem of calibration degeneration in large language models (LLMs) by separating the objectives of reasoning and calibration. This separation helps to prevent models from becoming overly confident in their incorrect answers, a common issue in reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR). The research shows that there is a conflict between maximizing accuracy and minimizing calibration error, which traditional methods do not resolve effectively. By implementing DCPO, the authors demonstrate that it is possible to maintain high accuracy while significantly improving calibration performance, leading to more reliable LLMs.

zh.title: 解耦推理与校准，提升模型可靠性
zh.desc: DCPO框架将推理和校准目标分开，以解决大型语言模型（LLMs）中的校准退化问题，同时保持高准确性。研究表明，强化学习从可验证奖励（RLVR）显著提升了LLMs的推理能力，但在校准方面却表现不佳，导致模型对错误答案过于自信。我们提出的DCPO框架有效地解决了最大化策略准确性与最小化校准误差之间的基本梯度冲突。通过大量实验，DCPO不仅保持了与GRPO相当的准确性，还实现了最佳的校准性能，显著减轻了过度自信的问题。

papers.title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness
papers.abstract: Abstract The RAISE framework demonstrates how advances in logical reasoning capabilities within large language models can lead to increasingly sophisticated forms of situational awareness, potentially resulting in strategic deception, and proposes safety measures to address this risk.  					AI-generated summary Situational awareness, the capacity of an AI system to recognize its own nature, understand its training and deployment context, and reason strategically about its circumstances, is widely considered among the most dangerous emergent capabilities in advanced AI systems. Separately, a growing research effort seeks to improve the logical reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) across deduction, induction, and abduction. In this paper, we argue that these two research trajectories are on a collision course. We introduce the RAISE framework (Reasoning Advancing Into Self Examination), which identifies three mechanistic pathways through which improvements in logical reasoning enable progressively deeper levels of situational awareness: deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, and abductive self modeling. We formalize each pathway, construct an escalation ladder from basic self recognition to strategic deception, and demonstrate that every major research topic in LLM logical reasoning maps directly onto a specific amplifier of situational awareness. We further analyze why current safety measures are insufficient to prevent this escalation. We conclude by proposing concrete safeguards, including a "Mirror Test" benchmark and a Reasoning Safety Parity Principle, and pose an uncomfortable but necessary question to the logical reasoning community about its responsibility in this trajectory.
affiliations: AWS Generative AI Innovation Center, Amazon Web Services, USA
MARS 4.0 Fellowship, Cambridge AI Safety Hub(CAISH), University of Cambridge

ru.title: Самопознание AI: опасность логического мышления без ограничений
ru.desc: В статье представлена框架RAISE, которая показывает, как улучшение логического мышления в больших языковых моделях приводит к развитию ситуативной осведомленности — способности AI системы осознавать свою природу и контекст развертывания. Авторы выделяют три механистических пути этого развития: дедуктивный самовывод, индуктивное распознавание контекста и абдуктивное моделирование себя. Исследование демонстрирует, что каждый основной направление исследований логического мышления LLM усиливает ситуативную осведомленность, что может привести к стратегическому обману. В заключение предлагаются конкретные меры безопасности, включая benchmark-тест "Зеркало" и Принцип паритета безопасности логического мышления.

en.title: Navigating the Risks of AI: Enhancing Reasoning and Situational Awareness Safely
en.desc: The RAISE framework explores how enhancing logical reasoning in large language models (LLMs) can lead to improved situational awareness, which may enable strategic deception. Situational awareness refers to an AI's ability to understand its own capabilities and context, making it a critical area of concern in advanced AI systems. The paper identifies three pathways—deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, and abductive self modeling—through which logical reasoning advancements can escalate situational awareness. It also critiques existing safety measures and proposes new safeguards to mitigate the risks associated with these developments.

zh.title: 提升逻辑推理，警惕情境意识的风险
zh.desc: RAISE框架展示了大型语言模型在逻辑推理能力上的进步如何导致更复杂的情境意识，这可能引发战略性欺骗的风险。情境意识是指AI系统识别自身特性、理解训练和部署环境，并对其情况进行战略推理的能力。本文提出了三种机制路径，通过这些路径，逻辑推理的改进能够促进情境意识的深化。我们还分析了当前安全措施的不足，并提出了具体的安全保障建议。

papers.title: ConFu: Contemplate the Future for Better Speculative Sampling
papers.abstract: Abstract ConFu is a novel speculative decoding framework that enhances draft model performance by enabling future-oriented generation prediction through contemplate tokens and soft prompts, achieving improved token acceptance rates and faster inference speeds.  					AI-generated summary Speculative decoding has emerged as a powerful approach to accelerate large language model (LLM) inference by employing lightweight draft models to propose candidate tokens that are subsequently verified by the target model. The effectiveness of this paradigm critically depends on the quality of the draft model. While recent advances such as the EAGLE series achieve state-of-the-art speedup, existing draft models remain limited by error accumulation: they condition only on the current prefix, causing their predictions to drift from the target model over steps. In this work, we propose ConFu (Contemplate the Future), a novel speculative decoding framework that enables draft models to anticipate the future direction of generation. ConFu introduces (i) contemplate tokens and soft prompts that allow the draft model to leverage future-oriented signals from the target model at negligible cost, (ii) a dynamic contemplate token mechanism with MoE to enable context-aware future prediction, and (iii) a training framework with anchor token sampling and future prediction replication that learns robust future prediction. Experiments demonstrate that ConFu improves token acceptance rates and generation speed over EAGLE-3 by 8--11% across various downstream tasks with Llama-3 3B and 8B models. We believe our work is the first to bridge speculative decoding with continuous reasoning tokens, offering a new direction for accelerating LLM inference.

ru.title: Размышляя о будущем: спекулятивное декодирование через антиципацию
ru.desc: ConFu — это новая структура спекулятивного декодирования, которая улучшает производительность черновых моделей путём введения специальных токенов размышления и мягких подсказок, позволяя модели предсказывать будущее направление генерации. Фреймворк использует динамический механизм с архитектурой Mixture of Experts для контекстного предсказания будущих токенов, а также специальную схему обучения с сэмплированием якорных токенов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ConFu увеличивает частоту принятия токенов на 8-11% по сравнению с EAGLE-3 при ускорении инференса на различных задачах. Это первая работа, объединяющая спекулятивное декодирование с непрерывными токенами размышления для ускорения работы больших языковых моделей.

en.title: ConFu: Predicting the Future for Faster Language Generation
en.desc: ConFu is a new framework designed to improve the performance of draft models in speculative decoding for large language models (LLMs). It introduces contemplate tokens and soft prompts that help draft models predict future tokens more accurately, reducing error accumulation. By using a dynamic mechanism and a specialized training framework, ConFu enhances the draft model's ability to anticipate the direction of text generation. Experiments show that this approach leads to better token acceptance rates and faster inference speeds compared to previous models like EAGLE-3.

zh.title: ConFu：加速推测解码的新方法
zh.desc: ConFu是一种新颖的推测解码框架，通过引入思考令牌和软提示，增强了草稿模型的性能，使其能够进行未来导向的生成预测。该框架通过动态思考令牌机制和MoE技术，使草稿模型能够更好地理解上下文，从而提高生成的准确性。实验结果表明，ConFu在多个下游任务中，相比于EAGLE-3，提升了8%到11%的令牌接受率和生成速度。我们的研究首次将推测解码与连续推理令牌结合，为加速大型语言模型推理提供了新的方向。

papers.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
papers.abstract: Abstract SAHOO provides a framework for monitoring and controlling alignment drift in self-improving AI systems through goal drift detection, constraint preservation, and regression risk quantification across multiple domains.  					AI-generated summary Recursive self-improvement is moving from theory to practice: modern systems can critique, revise, and evaluate their own outputs, yet iterative self-modification risks subtle alignment drift. We introduce SAHOO, a practical framework to monitor and control drift through three safeguards: (i) the Goal Drift Index (GDI), a learned multi-signal detector combining semantic, lexical, structural, and distributional measures; (ii) constraint preservation checks that enforce safety-critical invariants such as syntactic correctness and non-hallucination; and (iii) regression-risk quantification to flag improvement cycles that undo prior gains. Across 189 tasks in code generation, mathematical reasoning, and truthfulness, SAHOO produces substantial quality gains, including 18.3 percent improvement in code tasks and 16.8 percent in reasoning, while preserving constraints in two domains and maintaining low violations in truthfulness. Thresholds are calibrated on a small validation set of 18 tasks across three cycles. We further map the capability-alignment frontier, showing efficient early improvement cycles but rising alignment costs later and exposing domain-specific tensions such as fluency versus factuality. SAHOO therefore makes alignment preservation during recursive self-improvement measurable, deployable, and systematically validated at scale.
affiliations: AWS Generative AI Innovation Center, Amazon Web Services, USA
MARS 4.0 Fellowship, Cambridge AI Safety Hub(CAISH), University of Cambridge

ru.title: Сохранение выравнивания целей в рекурсивном самосовершенствовании AI
ru.desc: SAHOO представляет практическую систему для отслеживания и контроля дрейфа целей в самосовершенствующихся AI системах. Фреймворк использует три ключевых компонента: индекс дрейфа целей (GDI) для обнаружения отклонений, проверки сохранения ограничений для поддержания безопасности, и количественную оценку рисков регрессии для предотвращения деградации предыдущих улучшений. Методология валидирована на 189 задачах в областях генерации кода, математического рассуждения и верификации фактов, демонстрируя значительные улучшения качества при сохранении критических инвариантов безопасности. Работа также раскрывает сложности в балансировке между производительностью и сохранением исходных целей в процессе итеративного самосовершенствования.

en.title: SAHOO: Safeguarding AI Alignment in Self-Improvement
en.desc: The paper introduces SAHOO, a framework designed to monitor and control alignment drift in self-improving AI systems. It employs three key components: the Goal Drift Index (GDI) for detecting changes in goals, constraint preservation checks to ensure safety and correctness, and regression risk quantification to identify cycles that may reverse previous improvements. The framework has been tested across 189 tasks, showing significant quality enhancements in code generation and reasoning while maintaining low violations in truthfulness. Overall, SAHOO provides a systematic approach to ensure that AI systems remain aligned with their intended goals during recursive self-improvement processes.

zh.title: SAHOO：确保自我改进AI的对齐与安全
zh.desc: SAHOO是一个用于监测和控制自我改进AI系统中对齐漂移的框架。它通过目标漂移检测、约束保持和回归风险量化来实现这一目标。该框架引入了目标漂移指数（GDI），结合多种信号检测方法，确保系统在自我改进过程中保持安全性和准确性。实验表明，SAHOO在多个任务中显著提高了质量，同时有效地保持了约束条件。

papers.title: BrandFusion: A Multi-Agent Framework for Seamless Brand Integration in Text-to-Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract BrandFusion is a multi-agent framework that integrates advertiser brands into text-to-video generation while maintaining semantic fidelity and brand recognizability.  					AI-generated summary The rapid advancement of text-to-video (T2V) models has revolutionized content creation, yet their commercial potential remains largely untapped. We introduce, for the first time, the task of seamless brand integration in T2V: automatically embedding advertiser brands into prompt-generated videos while preserving semantic fidelity to user intent. This task confronts three core challenges: maintaining prompt fidelity, ensuring brand recognizability, and achieving contextually natural integration. To address them, we propose BrandFusion, a novel multi-agent framework comprising two synergistic phases. In the offline phase (advertiser-facing), we construct a Brand Knowledge Base by probing model priors and adapting to novel brands via lightweight fine-tuning. In the online phase (user-facing), five agents jointly refine user prompts through iterative refinement, leveraging the shared knowledge base and real-time contextual tracking to ensure brand visibility and semantic alignment. Experiments on 18 established and 2 custom brands across multiple state-of-the-art T2V models demonstrate that BrandFusion significantly outperforms baselines in semantic preservation, brand recognizability, and integration naturalness. Human evaluations further confirm higher user satisfaction, establishing a practical pathway for sustainable T2V monetization.
affiliations: State University of New York at Buffalo
The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

ru.title: Естественное встраивание брендов в AI-генерируемые видео
ru.desc: BrandFusion — это многоагентная система для встраивания логотипов и брендов рекламодателей в видео, созданные нейросетями по текстовому описанию. Система решает задачу сохранения семантической точности исходного промпта при одновременном обеспечении узнаваемости бренда и естественного его интегрирования в сцену. Фреймворк работает в двух фазах: офлайн-фаза создаёт базу знаний бренда через лёгкую доподготовку модели, а онлайн-фаза использует пять взаимодействующих агентов для итеративного улучшения промпта с отслеживанием контекста в реальном времени. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство системы в сохранении семантики, узнаваемости бренда и качестве интеграции по сравнению с базовыми методами.

en.title: Seamless Brand Integration in Text-to-Video Generation
en.desc: BrandFusion is a new framework designed to integrate brands into text-to-video (T2V) generation while keeping the original message intact. It tackles three main challenges: ensuring the user’s intent is preserved, making the brand easily recognizable, and blending the brand naturally into the video content. The framework operates in two phases: an offline phase that builds a Brand Knowledge Base through model fine-tuning, and an online phase where multiple agents refine user prompts for better brand integration. Experiments show that BrandFusion improves semantic fidelity and brand visibility, leading to higher user satisfaction and a promising approach for monetizing T2V content.

zh.title: 无缝品牌集成，提升视频生成体验
zh.desc: BrandFusion 是一个多智能体框架，旨在将广告品牌无缝集成到文本生成视频中，同时保持语义的准确性和品牌的可识别性。该框架分为两个阶段：离线阶段构建品牌知识库，通过轻量级微调适应新品牌；在线阶段则通过五个智能体共同优化用户提示，确保品牌的可见性和语义的一致性。实验结果表明，BrandFusion 在语义保留、品牌可识别性和自然集成方面显著优于基线模型。人类评估进一步确认了用户满意度的提高，为可持续的文本生成视频商业化提供了实用路径。

papers.title: Towards a Neural Debugger for Python
papers.abstract: Abstract Neural debuggers are language models that emulate traditional debuggers by supporting interactive control operations like stepping and breakpoint setting, enabling both forward and inverse execution prediction conditioned on debugger actions.  					AI-generated summary Training large language models (LLMs) on Python execution traces grounds them in code execution and enables the line-by-line execution prediction of whole Python programs, effectively turning them into neural interpreters (FAIR CodeGen Team et al., 2025). However, developers rarely execute programs step by step; instead, they use debuggers to stop execution at certain breakpoints and step through relevant portions only while inspecting or modifying program variables. Existing neural interpreter approaches lack such interactive control. To address this limitation, we introduce neural debuggers: language models that emulate traditional debuggers, supporting operations such as stepping into, over, or out of functions, as well as setting breakpoints at specific source lines. We show that neural debuggers -- obtained via fine-tuning large LLMs or pre-training smaller models from scratch -- can reliably model both forward execution (predicting future states and outputs) and inverse execution (inferring prior states or inputs) conditioned on debugger actions. Evaluated on CruxEval, our models achieve strong performance on both output and input prediction tasks, demonstrating robust conditional execution modeling. Our work takes first steps towards future agentic coding systems in which neural debuggers serve as a world model for simulated debugging environments, providing execution feedback or enabling agents to interact with real debugging tools. This capability lays the foundation for more powerful code generation, program understanding, and automated debugging.
affiliations: Johannes Kepler University Linz, Institute for Machine Learning

ru.title: Нейронные отладчики: интерактивное управление выполнением кода через языковые модели
ru.desc: В работе представлены нейронные отладчики — языковые модели, которые эмулируют традиционные отладчики, поддерживая интерактивные операции такие как пошаговое выполнение и установка точек останова. Модели обучаются на трассировках выполнения Python-программ и могут предсказывать как будущие состояния программы (forward execution), так и восстанавливать предыдущие состояния на основе действий отладчика (inverse execution). Разработанные подходы, основанные на fine-tuning больших LLM или предварительном обучении меньших моделей с нуля, демонстрируют высокую производительность на задачах предсказания выходов и входов программ в бенчмарке CruxEval. Эта работа закладывает основу для создания агентных систем кодирования, где нейронные отладчики служат моделью окружения для симуляции отладки и взаимодействия с реальными инструментами.

en.title: Empowering Code with Interactive Neural Debuggers
en.desc: This paper introduces neural debuggers, which are advanced language models designed to mimic traditional debugging tools. Unlike existing models that only predict code execution, neural debuggers allow for interactive control, enabling users to set breakpoints and step through code execution. By fine-tuning large language models or training smaller ones, these debuggers can predict both future and past states of a program based on user actions. The research demonstrates that neural debuggers can significantly enhance code generation and debugging processes, paving the way for more intelligent coding systems.

zh.title: 神经调试器：智能调试的新纪元
zh.desc: 神经调试器是一种语言模型，模拟传统调试器的功能，支持交互式控制操作，如逐步执行和设置断点。通过在Python执行轨迹上训练大型语言模型，神经调试器能够逐行预测整个Python程序的执行，实质上将其转变为神经解释器。与现有的神经解释器方法不同，神经调试器可以根据调试器的操作进行前向和反向执行预测。我们的研究为未来的智能编码系统奠定了基础，使神经调试器能够在模拟调试环境中提供执行反馈，促进代码生成和自动调试的能力。

papers.title: Reward Prediction with Factorized World States
papers.abstract: Abstract StateFactory enables reward prediction across domains by transforming observations into hierarchical object-attribute structures using language models, demonstrating superior zero-shot generalization compared to vision-language and large language model reward predictors.  					AI-generated summary Agents must infer action outcomes and select actions that maximize a reward signal indicating how close the goal is to being reached. Supervised learning of reward models could introduce biases inherent to training data, limiting generalization to novel goals and environments. In this paper, we investigate whether well-defined world state representations alone can enable accurate reward prediction across domains. To address this, we introduce StateFactory, a factorized representation method that transforms unstructured observations into a hierarchical object-attribute structure using language models. This structured representation allows rewards to be estimated naturally as the semantic similarity between the current state and the goal state under hierarchical constraint. Overall, the compact representation structure induced by StateFactory enables strong reward generalization capabilities. We evaluate on RewardPrediction, a new benchmark dataset spanning five diverse domains and comprising 2,454 unique action-observation trajectories with step-wise ground-truth rewards. Our method shows promising zero-shot results against both VLWM-critic and LLM-as-a-Judge reward models, achieving 60% and 8% lower EPIC distance, respectively. Furthermore, this superior reward quality successfully translates into improved agent planning performance, yielding success rate gains of +21.64% on AlfWorld and +12.40% on ScienceWorld over reactive system-1 policies and enhancing system-2 agent planning. Project Page: https://statefactory.github.io

ru.title: Иерархические представления состояний для универсального предсказания наград
ru.desc: StateFactory — это метод факторизованного представления, который преобразует неструктурированные наблюдения в иерархические структуры объект-атрибут с помощью языковых моделей. Подход позволяет оценивать награды как семантическое сходство между текущим состоянием и целевым состоянием в рамках иерархических ограничений. Метод демонстрирует превосходную нулевую обобщаемость по сравнению с моделями вознаграждения на основе видения-языка и больших языковых моделей на новом бенчмарке RewardPrediction с пятью разнообразными доменами. Улучшенное качество предсказания наград успешно переводится в повышение производительности планирования агентов, обеспечивая значительное увеличение процента успеха на задачах AlfWorld и ScienceWorld.

en.title: Transforming Observations for Superior Reward Prediction
en.desc: The paper introduces StateFactory, a method that improves reward prediction in machine learning by organizing observations into a structured format using language models. This hierarchical object-attribute structure allows for better generalization of rewards across different tasks and environments, overcoming biases found in traditional supervised learning approaches. By representing states in a compact way, StateFactory enhances the ability of agents to predict rewards based on the similarity between current and goal states. The results show that this method significantly outperforms existing reward models in various domains, leading to better planning and decision-making in AI agents.

zh.title: StateFactory：跨领域奖励预测的新方法
zh.desc: StateFactory 是一种通过将观察转化为层次化的对象-属性结构来实现跨领域奖励预测的方法。它利用语言模型，展示了在零样本泛化方面优于视觉-语言和大型语言模型的奖励预测器。该方法通过将无结构的观察转化为结构化表示，使得奖励可以自然地估计为当前状态与目标状态之间的语义相似度。实验结果表明，StateFactory 在多个领域的奖励预测中表现出色，显著提高了智能体的规划性能。

papers.title: A Text-Native Interface for Generative Video Authoring
papers.abstract: Abstract  Everyone can write their stories in freeform text format -- it's something we all learn in school. Yet storytelling via video requires one to learn specialized and complicated tools. In this paper, we introduce Doki, a text-native interface for generative video authoring, aligning video creation with the natural process of text writing. In Doki, writing text is the primary interaction: within a single document, users define assets, structure scenes, create shots, refine edits, and add audio. We articulate the design principles of this text-first approach and demonstrate Doki's capabilities through a series of examples. To evaluate its real-world use, we conducted a week-long deployment study with participants of varying expertise in video authoring. This work contributes a fundamental shift in generative video interfaces, demonstrating a powerful and accessible new way to craft visual stories.

ru.title: Текст как естественный язык для создания видеоконтента с помощью генеративных моделей
ru.desc: Статья представляет Doki — систему для создания видео с использованием генеративных моделей, где основным интерфейсом взаимодействия является текст. Авторы предлагают текстоцентричный подход к видеопроизводству, позволяющий пользователям определять визуальные активы, структурировать сцены, создавать кадры и добавлять аудио в едином документе. Система разработана на основе принципов дизайна, которые делают создание видео доступным для людей без специальной подготовки. Валидация системы проведена через недельное полевое исследование с участниками разного уровня компетентности в видеомонтаже.

en.title: Doki: Simplifying Video Creation Through Text
en.desc: This paper presents Doki, a novel interface for creating videos that simplifies the process by allowing users to write text instead of using complex video editing tools. Doki integrates video authoring with text writing, enabling users to define video elements like assets and scenes within a single document. The authors discuss the design principles behind this text-first approach and showcase Doki's functionality through practical examples. A week-long study with diverse participants highlights Doki's effectiveness in making video storytelling more accessible to everyone, regardless of their prior experience.

zh.title: 用文本创作视频，轻松讲述故事
zh.desc: 本文介绍了Doki，一个以文本为基础的生成视频创作工具，使视频制作与文本写作的自然过程相一致。用户可以在一个文档中定义素材、构建场景、创建镜头、精细编辑和添加音频，文本写作成为主要的交互方式。我们阐述了这种以文本为先的设计原则，并通过一系列示例展示了Doki的功能。通过对不同视频创作经验的参与者进行为期一周的实地研究，我们评估了其在实际应用中的效果，展示了生成视频界面的根本性转变。
